key_points: India's physical landscape is divided into six major physiographic divisions
The Himalayan Mountains stretch from the Indus river in the west to the Brahmaputra river in the east
The Himalayas consist of three parallel ranges: Himadri, Himachal and Shiwaliks
Himadri or the Greater Himalayas contain the highest peaks with permanent snow
Himachal or Lesser Himalayas contain famous valleys and hill stations
Shiwaliks are the outermost range made of unconsolidated sediments
The Northern Plains are formed by alluvial deposits brought by the Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers
The plains are divided into Bhabar, Terai, Bhangar and Khadar regions
The Peninsular Plateau is one of the oldest landmasses of the earth
It is divided into Central Highlands and the Deccan Plateau
The Western Ghats are higher and more continuous than the Eastern Ghats
The Indian Desert lies west of the Aravali Hills and has sandy plains and sand dunes
The coastal plains are divided into western coastal plains and eastern coastal plains
The western coastal plains include Konkan, Kannad and Malabar coasts
The eastern coastal plains include Northern Circar and Coromandel Coast
India has two island groups: Lakshadweep in the Arabian Sea and Andaman and Nicobar in the Bay of Bengal
examples: Example of a Himalayan peak: Kanchenjunga
Example of Himalayan valley: Kullu Valley
Example of plateau rich in minerals: Chotanagpur Plateau
Example of a desert river: Luni River
Example of western coastal region: Malabar Coast
Example of eastern coastal delta: Godavari delta
Example of coral island: Lakshadweep Islands
Example of active volcano island: Barren Island
formulas: Northern Plains formation = Alluvial deposits from Indus + Ganga + Brahmaputra
Peninsular Plateau divisions = Central Highlands + Deccan Plateau
Western Coastal Plains = Konkan + Kannad + Malabar
Eastern Coastal Plains = Northern Circar + Coromandel
exam_tips: Understand the structure and formation of the Himalayas
Remember the four relief divisions of the Northern Plains
Focus on the geological importance of the Peninsular Plateau
Learn the differences between Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats
Study the important coastal regions and river deltas
Remember the location and features of India's island groups
